acceptance.criterion: The snapshot handles authenticated and unauthenticated installs honestly, records unavailable capabilities with reasons, and never contains credentials or raw OAuth output.
acceptance.evidence: RouteDecisionMatrix snapshot/auth fixtures and the 2026-07-16 live 0.2.101 safe snapshot pass; only normalized facts and categorical reasons are serialized.

acceptance.criterion: The capability ledger proves the supported read-only, session, goal, streaming, schema, and ACP surfaces and explicitly identifies `--new-session` as unsupported.
acceptance.evidence: The live 0.2.101 ledger proves permission, read-only, session, streaming, schema, and ACP surfaces and records new_session=refuted:not_advertised_by_help.

acceptance.criterion: A model-free isolated `/goal status` probe using the narrow auth projection verifies goal-command resolution independently from catalog lookup and model inference.
acceptance.evidence: Live route-worker --probe-grok returned goal_command_resolution=proven from isolated_auth_projected_command_probe with no model events; focused probe tests pass.

acceptance.criterion: Batch 0 is additive: it changes no product launch argv or non-Grok adapter behavior.
acceptance.evidence: Capability inventory is isolated in worker_routing.py; adapter snapshot and routing regression tests preserve non-Grok launch behavior.

acceptance.criterion: Read-only, create, resume, and trusted full-run argv contain only installed-binary-supported flags, and new sessions use a caller-generated UUID through `--session-id`.
acceptance.evidence: Grok argv tests, exact UUID fixture coverage, installed --help evidence, and supervisor create/resume tests prove --session-id creation and exact --resume recovery without unsupported flags.

acceptance.criterion: OAuth launches retain private `HOME` and `GROK_HOME` plus the validated narrow `GROK_AUTH_PATH`; ambient config, SSH state, and git identity are not inherited.
acceptance.evidence: Full-run auth tests prove private HOME/GROK_HOME, owner-private GROK_AUTH_PATH projection, rotating OAuth continuity, and absence of ambient SSH/Git identity inheritance.

acceptance.criterion: XAI API-key behavior and every non-Grok adapter's emitted argv remain unchanged.
acceptance.evidence: API-key and adapter matrix tests cover XAI_API_KEY plus Codex, Claude, Devin, OpenCode, and fixture argv; the cumulative review found no non-Grok regression.

acceptance.criterion: A reduced-capability or unsupported Grok install fails or falls back with a concrete reason instead of entering an interactive login or invalid-flag loop.
acceptance.evidence: Reduced-help, unauthenticated, malformed-catalog, and missing-core-capability fixtures return typed fallback/failure reasons before spawn; no interactive-login retry path is emitted.

acceptance.criterion: Grok provider qualification is independent from goal support, and an unavailable goal capability selects the documented one-packet fallback without disabling an otherwise valid provider.
acceptance.evidence: The live authenticated provider selects grok_packet_prompt with goal_mode=false and reason goal_mode_not_behaviorally_verified; routing fixtures cover provider qualification independently.

acceptance.criterion: An isolated authenticated throwaway-repository canary records whether the packet-backed headless `/goal` launch reaches terminal state with the exact requested session identity; a complete terminal artifact enables goal mode, while an incomplete canary keeps goal mode disabled and proves selection of the one-packet fallback.
acceptance.evidence: The bounded 0.2.101 throwaway canary emitted work but did not reach an exact terminal event, so fallback remains selected; structured build/session/digest/terminal artifact validation tests pass.

acceptance.criterion: The streaming follow view exposes sanitized progress, usage, terminal state, and typed errors; it tolerates unknown event types and never requires timed driver narration.
acceptance.evidence: Twenty-three stream/report tests prove bounded sanitized progress, usage, terminal/errors, unknown-event tolerance, shared-OAuth suppression, and cross-record credential quarantine.

acceptance.criterion: Worker branch, commit, crash recovery, acceptance, protected-ref, PR, and merge authority remain identical to the provider-neutral full-run contract.
acceptance.evidence: Supervisor authority/crash/reconcile tests plus native feature-ancestry, origin-config, protected-ref, credential-strip, and narrow-Git-root tests preserve the provider-neutral boundary.

acceptance.criterion: Model selection uses the authenticated live catalog and parsed default; no unavailable model, including `auto` or `grok-code-fast-1`, is selected unless the catalog returns it.
acceptance.evidence: Real '-' and '*' catalog grammar fixtures plus the live 0.2.101 probe return grok-4.5 and grok-composer-2.5-fast, select the parsed default, and reject absent identifiers.

acceptance.criterion: A user can install, authenticate, capability-check, launch, follow, and recover the optional open-source Grok worker from one concise documentation path.
acceptance.evidence: references/grok-open-source-worker.md and the HTML guide cover install, auth, host-correct capability check, launch, follow, exact resume, and full-run-reconcile recovery; HTML parsing passes.

acceptance.criterion: SKILL, AGENTS, Claude/Codex references, README, guide, examples, changelog, and learnings agree on native-first routing, goal enhancement, one-packet fallback, and authority boundaries.
acceptance.evidence: Expanded repository consistency checks pass across SKILL, AGENTS, README, changelog, guide, setup/launch references, host parity, and learnings, including stale-policy rejection.

acceptance.criterion: Installed Codex and Claude Code bundle checks pass, and native worker routing remains parity-aligned, commit-capable, and authority-safe.
acceptance.evidence: Adaptive native-worker tests pass for Codex and Claude exact create/resume grammar, plan-matched effort, commit roots, stripped Git credentials, and terminal authority checks; final readiness runs both installed-bundle smokes.

continuation_guard.next_required_action: Run the single acceptance-bearing final-readiness proof, then remove only the operational session artifacts and attest the cleanup tip.
continuation_guard.reason: The user resumed the complete goal through reviewed-PR readiness.

master_acceptance.criterion: Against installed open-source Grok Build, the optional read-only and trusted full-run lanes emit no invalid flags and use capability-proven goal plus streaming behavior or the documented compatible fallback.
master_acceptance.evidence: Installed 0.2.101 capability evidence, exact argv/session tests, structured goal proof, sanitized stream regressions, and the live honest packet fallback cover both optional lanes.

master_acceptance.criterion: Authentication isolation, session create/resume identity, model selection, crash recovery, and worker authority match the executable and provider-neutral contracts exactly.
master_acceptance.evidence: Auth isolation, UUID lifecycle, live-catalog selection, resume/reconcile, feature ancestry, protected refs, and origin binding are covered by focused and supervisor tests plus cumulative review.

master_acceptance.criterion: Native Codex and Claude Code remain the default and parity-aligned, while all user-facing and installed Elves documentation accurately describes the optional open-source Grok path.
master_acceptance.evidence: Native-first policy and Claude/Codex parity are enforced by routing, consistency, native-worker, and installed-bundle gates; the concise Grok path is linked from canonical documentation.

master_acceptance.criterion: Focused proof, one cumulative final review, targeted revisions, and terminal repository checks leave a reviewed PR ready for the user without merging or moving a version tag.
master_acceptance.evidence: One cumulative root review and three independent reviews produced one consolidated revision; targeted delta proof is green and the canonical final-readiness command on this clean evidence-bearing tip supplies terminal proof before cleanup.
